articles.title: Trump's Iran Dilemma: Two Reasons Why US President Is Dragging His Feet On Peace Deal
articles.description: The ceasefire extension has exposed a deeper dilemma for Trump: how to pursue de-escalation with Iran without looking politically weak or surrendering strategic leverage

articles.title: Terror, Pakistan, Hormuz And UNSC: NSA Ajit Doval Shares Blunt Message To The World From Russia
articles.description: India’s NSA Ajit Doval delivered one of his toughest global security speeches yet at the first international security forum in Moscow. From Pakistan-sponsored terrorism to the Iran war, Hormuz blockade fears and the growing irrelevance of the United Nations, Doval fired warnings at multiple fronts simultaneously. He slammed double standards on terrorism, warned against using terror as state policy and questioned whether outdated global institutions can still handle modern crises. Doval also highlighted the massive risks of a wider Iran conflict and disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, through which nearly one-fifth of the world’s oil passes. The speech signalled a sharper, more assertive India willing to openly challenge Pakistan, question the UNSC structure and defend its strategic interests in an increasingly unstable world order. Moscow became the stage for India’s strongest security message yet on terrorism, global instability and geopolitical hypocrisy.

articles.title: Why Is the Trump Administration Investigating LinkedIn Co-Founder Reid Hoffman's Nonprofit?
articles.description: The Department of Justice is investigating Reid Hoffman's nonprofit, American Future Republic, due to its financial contributions that supported E. Jean Carroll's legal case against Trump. Hoffman has long been a major financial backer of the Democratic Party and its causes, donating millions over the years.

articles.title: NCB places habitual narcotics offender in prevention detention to curb drug-trafficking
articles.description: Referred to informally as a “scientist” by his peers in a nod to his technical prowess, Rahul Shedge, a post-graduate in organic chemistry, is allegedly adept at setting up facilities for the manufacturing of illicit drugs and allied chemicals.

articles.title: HC penalises man for harassing elderly mother through persistent litigation
articles.description: The Bombay High Court upheld a ruling favoring a mother over her son, imposing ₹50,000 in costs for his harassment during her twilight years.

articles.title: Supreme Court directs states, UTs to adopt a uniform trauma care policy
articles.description: The Supreme Court mandates a uniform trauma care policy nationwide, including a common helpline and protections for Good Samaritans, to improve emergency response.

articles.title: 'Aliens Walk Among Us': Trump White House Launches UFO-Style Immigration Tracker Website
articles.description: The Trump administration launched Aliens.gov, a UFO-themed immigration enforcement website tracking ICE arrests and migrant encounters nationwide. The site claims “aliens” are already living among Americans and encourages users to report “suspicious aliens” through an ICE tip line.

articles.title: Spectator by Seema Goswami: This is getting old
articles.description: Women in the public eye are shamed as they age naturally, and mocked when they don’t. It’s time to retire this hater energy

articles.description: India News: NEW DELHI: Trinamool Congress MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar on Thursday accused fellow party MP Kalyan Banerjee of \"verbally abusing\" her inside Lok Sabha .

articles.description: Traditionally confined to urban pockets and often perceived as a “party of traders”, the BJP is now attempting to broaden its social and political outreach

articles.title: Supreme Court grants divorce even as wife wanted adultery proven first
articles.description: The Supreme Court has dissolved a marriage despite the wife opposing divorce, not because she wanted to save the relationship, but because she first wanted to prove in court that her husband committed adultery.

articles.title: Helen Mirren called 'evil Zionist b****' in viral clip on night out with husband Taylor Hackford - WATCH
articles.description: Veteran actress Helen Mirren faced a viral social media incident where a Pro-Palestine protestor verbally accosted her and her husband, filmmaker Taylor Hackford, on a London street. The man, filming the couple, hurled accusations regarding Mirren's pro-Israel stance, referencing comments she made while promoting her film 'Golda'. The incident is reportedly being investigated as a potential hate crime.

articles.title: New Yorkers finally fed up with Mamdani
articles.description: A rally this week outside Gracie Mansion, the mayor's residence in New York City, highlights Mayor Zohran Mamdani's antisemitism problem: He doesn't think antisemitism is a problem.

articles.title: Cyprus’s desire to buy BrahMos, Kamikaze drones from India alarms Turkey
articles.description: Cyprus is seeking to acquire BrahMos cruise missiles and kamikaze drones from India, a move that has alarmed Türkiye. This potential defense deal, possibly part of an EU program, could significantly alter the regional military balance in the Eastern Mediterranean. The acquisition follows a roadmap for bilateral defense cooperation between India and Cyprus.

articles.title: TMC in disarray, Mamata Banerjee targets turncoats with ‘Girgit’ poem
articles.description: Calling the turncoats "more dangerous than a chameleon" for “betraying the party's foundational values and its workers in times of crisis for their own survival”, the former chief minister writes,

articles.title: Israel Faces Growing Threat From Hezbollah’s Fibre-Optic Kamikaze Drones
articles.description: Hezbollah's increasing deployment of fibre-optic drones along the Israel-Lebanon border is significantly altering the conflict dynamics, posing severe threats to Israeli military and civilian populations.

articles.title: The Constitution is my religion and voters are my abhimani devaru: Siddaramaiah
articles.description: Siddaramaiah vows to fight communal forces in Karnataka, emphasizing his commitment to the Constitution and voters after resigning as Chief Minister.

articles.description: Siddaramaiah and D.K. Shivakumar showcase enduring public camaraderie, navigating power dynamics within Congress with unity and warmth.

articles.title: Man stabs woman to death
articles.description: Chennai: Woman stabbed to death in Thiruverkadu over unpaid construction dues; accused mason arrested after jumping from second floor and suffering injuries.

articles.description: Chennai’s Triplicane saw pavements encroached by unauthorised TVK flags and DMK/TVK Bakrid banners, raising safety concerns; GCC to remove them.

articles.title: Iran seeks global order rejecting US hegemony, says top official
articles.description: Iran stated it is working to build a global order rejecting US "hegemony." A top official at a Moscow security forum also stressed that the strategic Strait of Hormuz cannot be allowed to become a source of insecurity for the country.

articles.title: Wrong side driving at GST rd at Saidapet puts motorists at risk
articles.description: Wrong-side driving surges on GST Road between Saidapet and Guindy under Chennai Metro pillars, raising crash risks; police plan barricades and peak-hour deployment.

articles.title: ‘Just left my second job this year due to having a boss who screams and throws things, is this the new norm?’: This viral story exposed what millions of American workers are too afraid to say
articles.description: The article discusses a viral story about a woman who quit two jobs due to abusive bosses, highlighting the alarming prevalence of workplace bullying in America and the effects it has on employees.

articles.title: End of an era of tall leaders who held sway across Karnataka
articles.description: The departure of Karnataka's tall leaders signals a pivotal shift in the state's political landscape and future dynamics.

articles.title: ‘US deserves mentally fit Prez’: ‘SLEEPY’ Trump faces backlash over dozing off during cabinet meet
articles.description: Donald Trump is facing massive backlash online after viral clips appeared to show him dozing off during a cabinet meeting, Memorial Day event, and White House appearances. Social media users revived the “Sleepy Joe” nickname once used against Joe Biden, with critics now mocking Trump as “Sleepy Donald” and “Slump Trump” amid growing scrutiny over his health and fitness. The controversy exploded after Trump seemingly nodded off while Marco Rubio discussed Iran, the Strait of Hormuz, and U.S. foreign policy during a high-stakes cabinet meeting.

articles.title: PM Modi congratulates new Slovenian PM Janez Jansa, eyes stronger ties
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Janez Jansa on his election as Slovenia's new PM, looking forward to strengthening bilateral ties. Slovenia's parliament voted to bring back the right-wing leader, ending a post-election deadlock.

articles.title: Compassionate appointment can’t be used for career growth: Why court denied relief to Uttarakhand man
articles.description: The Uttarakhand High Court was hearing a plea filed by a junior assistant challenging the state government’s decision rejecting his claim for appointment as a junior engineer.

articles.title: Tension At West Bengal Border As Migrants Wait For Return Amid Holding Centre Operations
articles.description: A tense situation has developed at the Hakimpur border in West Bengal’s North 24 Parganas, where dozens of undocumented Bangladeshi migrants are gathering to return home. Authorities are recording details and shifting many to holding centres, with over 350 already documented. Migrants cite fear of detention and increased checks in recent months. Some were brought illegally through agents years ago. While pushback plans are underway, the process has been temporarily halted, with uncertainty over Bangladesh’s acceptance of returnees.

articles.title: Even Non-Chinese Trade Depends on China | The Reason Why
articles.description: As tensions escalated around the Gulf region, many Western shipping companies rerouted their vessels via the Cape of Good Hope.

articles.title: 'Demolition threat looms over' Savarkar Sadan: Right-wing protests, demands national monument status for historic residence
articles.description: Coinciding with the birth anniversary of revolutionary freedom fighter and Hindutva ideologue Vinayak Damodar Savarkar, the Hindu Janajagruti Samiti (HJS) and several right-wing organisations staged a protest at Dadar on Thursday, demanding that the government acquire and preserve “Savarkar Sadan” at Shivaji Park as a national monument.

articles.title: The way Rahul treated Siddaramaiah is not right: Joshi
articles.description: Union minister Pralhad Joshi says Rahul Gandhi’s treatment of Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah was inappropriate, citing Congress infighting and governance issues.

articles.title: Trump’s Cuba Threat Escalates As US Warships Circle Caribbean, Havana Braces For Crisis
articles.description: Donald Trump’s latest remarks on Cuba have triggered fears of a dangerous new flashpoint just 90 miles from US shores. As American warships, surveillance aircraft, and Marine assault groups increase activity near the Caribbean, questions are growing over whether Washington is preparing direct action against Havana. With Marco Rubio calling Cuba a national security threat and the White House ramping up pressure, analysts now warn the situation could spiral into the biggest US-Cuba confrontation in decades. From Bay of Pigs memories to economic collapse inside Cuba, the crisis is reviving Cold War tensions across the region. Could Trump launch strikes on Cuba? Is this military pressure or preparation for something bigger? Watch the full breakdown.

articles.title: Iran backs Oman after Trump threatens to ‘blow them up’ over Hormuz dispute: Why Muskat finds itself at the centre of crisis
articles.description: The latest confrontation unfolded as fragile negotiations between Tehran and Washington over reopening the Strait of Hormuz appeared to be stalling once again.
